notes: GreenPort Logistics — ISO 14001 + EU EIA Directive environmental audit, Rotterdam port facility

ledger_entries.note: New cold storage facility H3 online mid-June — 45,000 kWh additional baseload
November bill delayed — Eneco meter reading scheduled for Dec 20

review_items.title: Nov-Dec 2025: Electricity and gas data pending
review_items.description: Last 2 months of energy data still outstanding. Eneco meter reading scheduled Dec 20. Required for complete Scope 1+2 calculations.

review_items.title: EU EIA Directive: Environmental Impact Statement not filed for H3 expansion
review_items.description: New cold storage facility H3 (8,200 m2) became operational June 2025 without Environmental Impact Assessment filing required under EU Directive 2011/92/EU Annex II.

review_items.title: Stormwater discharge: zinc concentration exceeds permit limit
review_items.description: Q3 2025 stormwater monitoring shows zinc at 0.18 mg/L vs. permit limit 0.15 mg/L. Source suspected: galvanized roof panels on Warehouse 2.

review_items.title: Scope 1 emissions increased 22% YoY due to fleet expansion
review_items.description: 2025 Scope 1 emissions projected at 4,850 tCO2e vs. 2024 baseline 3,975 tCO2e. 22% increase driven by 15 additional diesel trucks added in Q2.

review_items.title: ISO 14001 §8.1: Chemical storage non-conformity
review_items.description: Chemical inventory shows 3 incompatible chemical groups stored in same bund area (oxidizers + flammables). Violation of PGS 15 (Dutch hazardous substances storage guideline).

review_items.title: Noise monitoring: nighttime operations exceed 45 dB(A) at receptor R3
review_items.description: Q3 noise assessment shows 48 dB(A) Leq at nearest residential receptor vs. 45 dB(A) nighttime limit. Container handling operations 22:00-06:00 identified as source.

review_items.title: Scope 3 emissions: supply chain data coverage only 62%
review_items.description: Scope 3 Category 4 (upstream transport) and Category 9 (downstream transport) emissions based on spend-based estimates for 38% of suppliers. Activity data needed.

audit_log.detail: EMS manual and emissions inventory uploaded
Extraction complete — 1,220 data points from 8 documents

measures.title: Stormwater Treatment — Bioretention + Zinc Source Control
measures.description: Install bioretention system at Warehouse 2 stormwater outfall to treat zinc-contaminated runoff from galvanized roofing. Replace 2,400 m2 of galvanized roofing panels with coated aluminum during scheduled roof maintenance.

evidence.measurement: Stormwater sampling Q1-Q3 2025: zinc 0.14-0.18 mg/L (permit: 0.15 mg/L). Flow-weighted mean: 0.16 mg/L at outfall SW-03
evidence.nameplate: Proposed: Rocla Stormfilter bioretention cells (3 units, 12 m3 total), ZPG media. Roof replacement: 2,400 m2 Kalzip 65/400 aluminum panels
evidence.method: Bioretention zinc removal efficiency 75-85% (UNH Stormwater Center data). Cost avoidance: permit violation fines up to €50,000/incident + remediation costs. Roof lifecycle: aluminum adds 15 years vs. galvanized
evidence.price_basis: Bioretention system: €85,000 installed (Rocla Netherlands). Roof replacement: €95,000 (material + labor during planned maintenance). Permit violation: €50,000/incident (Waterwet enforcement)
evidence.confidence_note: Stormwater data from 3 quarters of monitoring. Bioretention efficiency from peer-reviewed UNH data — site-specific performance may vary with rainfall intensity

measures.title: Fleet Electrification — Phase 1 (8 Electric Trucks)
measures.description: Replace 8 oldest Euro V diesel trucks with DAF CF Electric 37t trucks for regional distribution (<200 km routes). Install 4 DC fast chargers (150 kW) at depot.

evidence.measurement: Fleet telemetry 2025: 8 target trucks avg 65,000 km/year, 32 L/100km diesel, total 166,400 L/year. CO2: 442 tCO2e/year (Scope 1)
evidence.nameplate: Replacing: 8x DAF CF 340 Euro V (2016-2017). With: 8x DAF CF Electric 37t, 350 kWh battery, 200 km range. Chargers: 4x ABB Terra 184 (150 kW DC)
evidence.method: Diesel cost: 166,400 L × €1.65/L = €274,560. Electric cost: 320,000 kWh × €0.18/kWh = €57,600. Maintenance saving: €32,000/year. Net: €185,000/year + 442 tCO2e Scope 1 reduction
evidence.price_basis: Diesel: Shell fleet card €1.65/L (2025). Electricity: Eneco commercial €0.18/kWh. DAF CF Electric: €185,000/unit ex-works. ABB Terra 184: €65,000/unit installed
evidence.confidence_note: Route analysis shows 85% of selected trucks' routes are <180 km. Winter range reduction 15-20% may require occasional diesel backup. TCO assumes 8-year vehicle lifecycle

measures.title: Nighttime Noise Mitigation — Container Handling Zone
measures.description: Install 4m noise barrier (acoustic fencing) along eastern boundary of container yard and retrofit rubber dampening pads on 6 reach stackers to reduce ground-borne vibration and impact noise during nighttime operations.

evidence.measurement: Noise monitoring Q3 2025 (Brüel & Kjær 2250): 48 dB(A) Leq at receptor R3 (22:00-06:00), 65 dB(A) LAmax during container stacking. Dominant source: reach stacker operations
evidence.nameplate: Proposed: 180m × 4m Kokowall noise barrier (Rw 28 dB). 6x Kalmar DRF 450-65S reach stackers retrofitted with Trelleborg rubber dampening pads
evidence.method: Acoustic modeling (SoundPLAN 9.0): barrier provides 8 dB insertion loss at R3, reducing to 40 dB(A). Rubber pads reduce LAmax by 6 dB. Compliance with Activiteitenbesluit nighttime limit 45 dB(A)
evidence.price_basis: Kokowall barrier: €350/linear meter installed = €63,000. Trelleborg pads: €5,300/stacker × 6 = €31,800. Total: €94,800. Cost avoidance: enforcement order + operating restriction estimated at €28,000/year
evidence.confidence_note: Noise measurements by certified acoustician. SoundPLAN model validated against daytime measurements (±2 dB). Barrier effectiveness depends on weather conditions (wind direction)
